fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 63 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the branch that restricts y to [low, high] should be entered; the condition `not clamp` is inverted so this check is FALSE on failing tests.
The then-branch (which applies clamping) should only be reached when clamp is False due to the inverted condition; this exposes the logic inversion.

spec.expr: y__2 >= min(y0, y1) and y__2 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After clamping, y__2 must lie within the segment range [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
The else-branch (no clamping applied) should only be reached when clamp is False; due to the bug it is reached when clamp is True.
